# Configure service discovery

You have two options for service discovery:

- Consul service discovery, which requires access to a Consul cluster. Consul is
  the default option for service discovery.
- Nomad service discovery, which requires no additional infrastructure.

Refer to the [Service discovery in Nomad
documentation](/nomad/docs/networking/service-discovery) for a comparison of
Nomad and Consul service discovery options.

## Consul service discovery

In both options, use the [`service`
block](/nomad/docs/job-specification/service), with the [`provider` parameter](/nomad/docs/job-specification/service#provider)
declaring the service discovery option to use.  You may omit the `provider` parameter because `consul` is the default service
discovery provider.

If you are using Consul for service discovery and have not enabled service mesh
features, then use the DNS to discover services and nodes in the Consul catalog.

`<service_name>.service.<consul_datacenter>.<consul_domain>`

Consul's default datacenter is `dc1` and default domain is `consul`. Those
values are configurable, so verify those values with your Consul admin.

This example illustrates an application with a web UI that connects to a backend
service. It assumes you are running Nomad and Consul on AWS EC2 instances. 

https://github.com/hashicorp/demo-consul-101/blob/master/services/dashboard-service/README.md

<details>
<summary style={{ cursor: "pointer" }}>
  Expand to review the full <code>countdash.nomad.hcl</code> job specification.
</summary>

<CodeBlockConfig lineNumbers="true" filename="countdash.nomad.hcl">

```hcl
variable "countdash-api-port" {
  description = "Countdash API Port"
  default = 9001
}
variable "countdash-web-port" {
  description = "Countdash web port"
  default = 9002
}

job "countdash" {

  group "countdash-api" {
    count = 1

    network {
      port "countdash-api" {
        static = var.countdash-api-port
      }
      dns {
      	servers = ["172.17.0.1"] 
      }
    }

    service {
      name = "countdash-api"
      provider = "consul"
      port = "countdash-api"      
      address  = attr.unique.platform.aws.local-ipv4
      # address = attr.unique.network.ip-address

      check {
        name     = "Countdash API ready" 
        type     = "http"
        path     = "/actuator/health"
        interval  = "5s"
				timeout   = "5s"

        check_restart {
          limit = 0
        }
      }      
    }

    task "countdash-api" {
      driver = "docker"

      constraint {
        attribute = "${meta.nodeRole}"
        operator  = "!="
        value     = "ingress"
      }

      meta {
        service = "countdash-api"
      }

      config {
        image = "hashicorpdev/counter-api:v3"
        ports = ["countdash-api"]
        mount {
          type   = "bind"
          source = "local/application.properties"
          target = "/application.properties"
        }
      }
      template {
        data = "server.port=${var.countdash-api-port}"
        destination = "local/application.properties"
      }
      resources {
        memory = 500
      }
    }
  }

  group "countdash-web" {

    count = 1

    network {
      port "countdash-web" {
        static = var.countdash-web-port
      }
      dns {
      	servers = ["172.17.0.1"] 
      }
    }

    service {
      name = "countdash-web"
      provider = "consul"
      port = "countdash-web"
      address  = attr.unique.platform.aws.public-hostname
      check {
        name      = "Countdash web ready"
        type      = "http"
        path			= "/"
        interval  = "5s"
        timeout   = "5s"
      }
    }

    task "countdash-web" {
      driver = "docker"
      constraint {
        attribute = "${meta.nodeRole}"
        operator  = "="
        value     = "ingress"
      }
      meta {
        service = "countdash-web"
      }
      env {
        COUNTING_SERVICE_URL = "http://countdash-api.service.dc1.consul:${var.countdash-api-port}"
        PORT="${var.countdash-web-port}"
      }

      config {
        image          = "hashicorpdev/counter-dashboard:v3"
        auth_soft_fail = true
        ports = ["countdash-web"]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

</details>

## Nomad service discovery

This section illustrates Nomad service discovery using parts of the Countdash
example job specification. The application has a frontend `countdash-web`
service that communicates with a backend `countdash-api` service.

You may view the complete job spec in the
[Countdash job specification section](#countdash-job-specification).

### Declare the service provider

Because Consul is the default service provider, you must configure the `service`
block's [`provider` parameter](/nomad/docs/job-specification/service#provider)
to use Nomad service discovery.  

<CodeBlockConfig lineNumbers="true" highlight="4" filename="countdash.nomad.hcl">

```hcl
job "countdash" {
  group "countdash-api" {
    service {
      provider = "nomad"
    }
  }
}
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

### Configure health checks

Define health checks with the [`check`
block](/nomad/docs/job-specification/check) to make sure that the service
catalog only returns healthy instances.

<Tabs>

<Tab heading="countdash-api">

<CodeBlockConfig lineNumbers="true" 
                 filename="countdash.nomad.hcl"
                 highlight="7-16" >

```hcl
job "countdash" {
  group "countdash-api" {
    service {
      name = "countdash-api"
      provider = "nomad"

      check {
        name     = "Countdash API ready" 
        type     = "http"
        path     = "/actuator/health"
        interval  = "5s"
        timeout   = "5s"

        check_restart {
          limit = 0
        }
      }          
    }
  }
}
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

</Tab>
<Tab heading="countdash-web">

<CodeBlockConfig lineNumbers="true" 
                 filename="countdash.nomad.hcl"
                 highlight="7-13" >

```hcl
job "countdash" {
  group "countdash-web" {
    service {
      name = "countdash-web"
      provider = "nomad"

      check {
        name      = "Countdash web ready"
        type      = "http"
        path			= "/"
        interval  = "5s"
        timeout   = "5s"
      }      
    }
  }
}
```

</CodeBlockConfig>
</Tab>
</Tabs>

### Configure access to other services

Use [`template`
blocks](/nomad/docs/job-specification/template#nomad-services) blocks with the
Consul Template [`nomadService`
function](/consul/docs/reference/consul-template/go#service) to locate services
in the Nomad service discovery catalog.

The `countdash-web` service needs to communicate with its backend
`countdash-api` service. The template block code searches for
`countdash-api` and assigns the result to the `COUNTING_SERVICE_URL` variable.

<CodeBlockConfig lineNumbers="true" 
                 filename="countdash.nomad.hcl"
                 highlight="4-13" >

```hcl
job "countdash" {
  group "countdash-web" {
    task "countdash-web" {
      template {
        data = <<EOH
        BIND_ADDRESS = ":${var.countdash-api-port}"
{{ range nomadService "countdash-api" }}
COUNTING_SERVICE_URL = "http://{{ .Address }}:{{ .Port }}"
{{ end }}
EOH
        destination = "local/env.txt"
        env         = true
      }
    }
  }
}
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

The `template` can then be used as a configuration file or have its content
loaded as environment variables to configure connection information in
applications.

### Countdash job specification

<details>
<summary style={{ cursor: "pointer" }}>
  Expand to review the full <code>countdash.nomad.hcl</code> job specification.
</summary>

This job specification deploys a job called "countdash" to a Nomad cluster
running on AWS. You may review the frontend and backend service code in the
[HashiCorp `demo-consul-101`
repository](https://github.com/hashicorp/demo-consul-101/blob/main/services/README.md).

The web application runs on port 9002, so make sure to open that port in your
AWS security group.

If you want to run the job on your localhost Nomad Linux installation with the
`sudo nomad agent -dev` command, you need to update each group's
`service.address` parameter value to `attr.unique.network.ip-address`.


<CodeBlockConfig lineNumbers="true" 
                 filename="countdash.nomad.hcl"
                 highlight="23,27-38,78,82-88,107-116">

```hcl
variable "countdash-api-port" {
  description = "Countdash API Port"
  default = 9001
}
variable "countdash-web-port" {
  description = "Countdash web port"
  default = 9002
}

job "countdash" {

  group "countdash-api" {
    count = 1

    network {
      port "countdash-api" {
        static = var.countdash-api-port
      }
    }

    service {
      name = "countdash-api"
      provider = "nomad"
      port = "countdash-api"      
      address  = attr.unique.platform.aws.local-ipv4

      check {
        name      = "Countdash API ready" 
        type      = "http"
        path      = "/actuator/health"
        interval  = "5s"
        timeout   = "5s"

        check_restart {
          limit = 0
        }
      }          
     }    

    task "countdash-api" {
      driver = "docker"      

      meta {
        service = "countdash-api"
      }

      config {
        image = "hashicorpdev/counter-api:v3"
        ports = ["countdash-api"]
        mount {
          type   = "bind"
          source = "local/application.properties"
          target = "/application.properties"
        }
      }
      template {
        data = "server.port=${var.countdash-api-port}"
        destination = "local/application.properties"
      }
      resources {
        memory = 500
      }
    }
  }

  group "countdash-web" {

    count = 1

    network {
      port "countdash-web" {
        static = var.countdash-web-port
      }
    }

    service {
      name = "countdash-web"
      provider = "nomad"
      port = "countdash-web"
      address  = attr.unique.platform.aws.public-hostname

      check {
        name      = "Countdash web ready"
        type      = "http"
        path      = "/"
        interval  = "5s"
        timeout   = "5s"
      }
    }

    task "countdash-web" {
      driver = "docker"

      meta {
        service = "countdash-web"
      }
      env {
        PORT="${var.countdash-web-port}"
      }

      config {
        image          = "hashicorpdev/counter-dashboard:v3"
        auth_soft_fail = true
        ports = ["countdash-web"]
      }

      template {
        data = <<EOH
        BIND_ADDRESS = ":${var.countdash-api-port}"
{{ range nomadService "countdash-api" }}
COUNTING_SERVICE_URL = "http://{{ .Address }}:{{ .Port }}"
{{ end }}
EOH
        destination = "local/env.txt"
        env         = true
      }
    }
  }
}
```

</CodeBlockConfig>
</details>


### Find services

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="CLI">

Use the [`nomad service list` command](/nomad/commands/service/list) to fetch a list of services.

```shell-script
$ nomad service list
Service Name   Tags
countdash-api  []
countdash-web  []
```

Use the [`nomad service info` command](/nomad/commands/service/info) to fetch
details for a specific service. This example retrieves information about the
`countdash-web` service and formats the output as JSON. Note that the `Address`
value is the public IPv4 address.

```shell-script
$ nomad service info -json countdash-web
[
    {
        "Address": "ec2-3-21-113-32.us-east-2.compute.amazonaws.com",
        "AllocID": "b1d44744-1592-4241-e904-6a4654a40898",
        "CreateIndex": 849,
        "Datacenter": "dc1",
        "ID": "_nomad-task-b1d44744-1592-4241-e904-6a4654a40898-group-countdash-web-countdash-web-countdash-web",
        "JobID": "countdash",
        "ModifyIndex": 849,
        "Namespace": "default",
        "NodeID": "b348d8ac-1020-1b9a-fbb8-0d4a9dbeb5ca",
        "Port": 9002,
        "ServiceName": "countdash-web",
        "Tags": []
    }
]
```

</Tab>
<Tab heading="UI">

1. Access the **Jobs** screen.
1. Click the **countdash** job name. The job details screen opens to the **Overview** tab.
1. Click the **Services** tab to review a list of services deployed by the job.
1. Click **countdash-web** to view the public IP address. 

</Tab>
</Tabs>


## Use service tags

You may specify `service` blocks multiple times with the same name but for
different ports. All instances are returned when the service name is queried.
To restrict results, you may assign [`tags`][] to services to group them. When
querying the service, you may provide tag values as part of the service name.

This example exposes an application on two ports for different protocols.

```hcl
job "..." {
  # ...
  group "..." {
    network {
      port "http" {}
      port "grpc" {}
    }

    service {
      name = "my-app"
      port = "http"
      tags = ["http"]
      # ...
    }

    service {
      name = "my-app"
      port = "grpc"
      tags = ["grpc"]
      # ...
    }
  }
}
```

By assigning different tags, you may reach the port for each protocol with
the `http.my-app` and `grpc.my-app` service queries.

### Canary deployments

When using [canary][jobspec_update_canary] or
[blue/green][jobspec_update_blue_green] upgrades you can specify a different
set of tags for the canary allocations using the [`canary_tags`][]
configuration.

During a deployment, the new allocations are registered with the tags set in
`canary_tags` while non-canaries have the values in `tags`. Having different
sets of tags allow you to create separate [load balancing][learn_lb] routing
rules to preview canaries.

-> **Note**: Services are registered with either `tags` or `canary_tags`. In
  order to share values they must be set in both fields.
